name: All-Season Quick Bread or Muffins (Basic R
recipeIngredient: 2 cups Prepared Fruits (such as Banana, zucchini, carrot, tomato, orange, or apple)
recipeInstructions:
COMMENTS: Choose from the following fruit options for your quick bread or muffins:
- For bananas, peel and puree 4 to 5 medium bananas to make 2 cups.
- For carrots, grate 1 or more medium carrots to make 2 cups.
- For zucchini, shred 3 medium zucchinis to make 2 cups.
- For oranges, grate the rind of one orange and chop the fruit for 2 cups total.
- For apples, peel, core, and shred four medium apples; add 1 teaspoon lemon juice to the shredded apple.
- For tomatoes, peel three to four medium tomatoes, remove seeds, and chop into 2 cups.
For quick loaves: Prepare two greased loaf pans (8½ x 4½ x 2 inches).
For muffins: Prepare two 12-cup muffin pans.
1. In a medium bowl, mix together the whole wheat flour, all-purpose flour, salt, baking soda, baking powder, and cinnamon. If preparing a batch for storage, omit the walnuts and divide the dry ingredients into three batches.
2. In a large bowl, whisk eggs until foamy. Gradually add sugar, oil, and vanilla extract, then whisk until well blended.
3. Add the chosen fruit or vegetable to the wet mixture. Mix in the dry ingredients with a fork until just combined—do not overmix.
4. Divide the batter evenly among the prepared loaf pans or muffin pans.
5. For quick loaves: Bake at 350°F for 50-60 minutes, or until a toothpick inserted into the center comes out clean. Allow to cool in the pans for 10 minutes before transferring to wire racks.
6. When fully cooled, wrap the loaves tightly in aluminum foil and refrigerate for up to 24 hours before slicing.
7. For muffins: Bake at 375°F for 12-15 minutes (or until a toothpick inserted into the center comes out clean). Serve immediately.
